---
title: "FR-BCA: Word Tables"
author: "Juan F. Fung"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
site: bookdown::bookdown_site
documentclass: book
mainfont: Times New Roman
biblio-style: apalike
link-citations: yes
description: "This is a placeholder Word document to hold R-generated tables (and potentially figures) for use in Word journal article templates."
---

```{r include=FALSE}
## copied from officedown example
library(knitr)
library(officedown)
library(officer)
opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE, tab.lp="Table:", fig.lp="Figure:")
fp <- fp_par(text.align = "center", padding = 20, border.bottom = fp_border())
ft <- fp_text(font.size = 12, bold = TRUE, color = "#C32900", font.family = "Times New Roman")

options(knitr.kable.NA = '')
## options(scipen=999, digits=3, knitr.table.format="latex")

## load reticulate and other packages
library(pacman)
pacman::p_load(
          "here",
          "devtools",
          "usethis",
          "tidyverse",
          "reticulate",
          "flextable",
          "kableExtra"
          )

## TODO: INCLUDE HELPER FUNCTION TO FORMAT NAMES IN PKG
my_name_repair <- function(name) {
  return(gsub('__days', '',
              tolower(gsub('(\\s+|\\(|\\?|-|/)', '_', gsub('\\)', '', name)))))
}


########################################################################
#### STEPS TO TEST AND LOAD LOCAL DEV VERSION OF PACKAGE ####
## 1. usethis::proj_activate(PATH)
## 2. devtools::load_all()
## 3. devtools::document()
## 4. devtools::check()
## 5. push to gh!

# Activate the frbca project from the parent directory
usethis::proj_activate(here::here())
path_frbca = here::here()

## load package
devtools::load_all(path_frbca)

## Source the analysis setup script that defines variables, runs analysis, and generates all figures/tables
source(here::here("analysis/scripts/run_analysis.R"))


########################################################################

## TO LOAD FROM LOCAL OR GITHUB:
## devtools::install_github(here('r/pkgs/frbca'))
## devtools::install_github("juanfung/frbca")

```


# Background

This document is written using the R package **bookdown** and R package **officedown**.
The goal is to generate Word tables using the `flextable` package.



<!-- ```{r load-data, echo=FALSE, include=FALSE} -->
<!-- ## path to inputs -->
<!-- path_inputs = here('data', 'processed', 'model-inputs', 'inspire') -->

<!-- input_eal = readr::read_csv(file.path(path_inputs, 'input-eal-inspire.csv'), -->
<!--                             name_repair=my_name_repair) %>% -->
<!--   dplyr::rename_with(~stringr::str_remove(., '__days')) -->

<!-- input_cost = readr::read_csv(file.path(path_inputs, 'input-cost-inspire.csv'), -->
<!--                              name_repair=my_name_repair) %>% -->
<!--   dplyr::rename(c_s = structural, c_ns = nonstructural) -->

<!-- input_param = readRDS(file.path(path_inputs, 'input-param-inspire.rds')) -->

<!-- ``` -->

<!-- ```{r frbca-output, echo=FALSE, include=FALSE} -->
<!-- ## run analysis and save output -->
<!-- output <- frbca::frbca(input_eal, input_cost, input_param) -->

<!-- ``` -->

<!-- ```{r tab.cap='rcmf-example', tab.id='rcmf-example', echo=FALSE} -->
<!-- ft <- output %>% -->
<!--   dplyr::filter(!is.na(bcr) & num_stories == 4 & label == "base") %>% -->
<!--   dplyr::select(model, delta_loss, benefit) %>% -->
<!--   dplyr::mutate_if(is.numeric, -->
<!--                    scales::dollar, -->
<!--                    accuracy=0.01) %>% -->
<!--   flextable::flextable() %>% -->
<!--   flextable::align(align="right", part="all") -->

<!-- ft <- flextable::set_table_properties(ft, width = 1, layout = "autofit") -->
<!-- ft -->


<!-- ``` -->
